channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Hamilton crashes out as Antonelli sets pace in final Belgium practice
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari crew face a race against time to repair his car for Belgian Grand Prix Qualifying after he crashed at the end of final practice, in a session topped again by Mercedes' title-leading Kimi Antonelli.

item.title: Badejo-Okusanya takes early lead in NBA presidential poll
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya takes an early lead in the NBA Presidential Election. Get the latest results and updates on the Nigerian Bar Association polls.
Read More: https://punchng.com/badejo-okusanya-takes-early-lead-in-nba-presidential-poll/

item.title: Timi Dakolo denies £4,106 debt claim, threatens legal action against DJ
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Timi Dakolo denies owing Revolve Entertainment £4,106 for a UK tour, with his lawyers threatening legal action against the promoter for defamation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/timi-dakolo-denies-4106-debt-claim-threatens-legal-action-against-dj/

item.title: Bayelsa CP hails Diri’s skills programme for reducing crime
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa State Commissioner of Police commends Governor Diri’s skills acquisition programme for significantly reducing crime by empowering youths.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bayelsa-cp-hails-diris-skills-programme-for-reducing-crime/

item.title: Crackdown at UNICAL: 8 Staff fired, one penalized as varsity fights malpractice
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Aniekan Aniekan, Calabar The Governing Council of the University of Calabar has dismissed eight members of staff and sanctioned another for gross misconduct, in what management described as a major step to enforce discipline and restore integrity in the institution. The decision, taken at the C

item.title: Iran hits second Kuwait power plant, Bahrain repels aerial attacks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Iran strikes another Kuwaiti power plant, causing outages and fires. Bahrain also repels aerial attacks amidst escalating regional tensions.
Read More: https://punchng.com/iran-hits-second-kuwait-power-plant-bahrain-repels-aerial-attacks/

item.title: Mary Habila Family Rejects Autopsy As Lawyer Seeks Transfer Of Investigation To Abuja
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The family of the late Mary Habila, a nurse attached to the office of the Minister of Works, Engr David Umahi, on Friday through its lawyer, submitted a petition at the Force Headquarters in Abuja against the Commissioner of Police in Ebonyi State, accusing the command of unlawfully withholding Habi

item.title: D’Tigers Assistant Captain Targets World Cup Place, Seeks To Uncover Basketball Talents
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: D’Tigers assistant captain Michael Eric has set his sights on helping Nigeria qualify for the 2027 FIBA Basketball World Cup, reports Completesports.com. David Fizdale’s side completed a flawless campaign at the African qualifiers in Luanda, Angola winning all three games. The impressive

item.title: MURIC Backs FRSC’s Anti-Bus Preaching, Hawking Bill
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A bill proposed by the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) which aims at banning preaching inside commercial buses and hawking has received the backing of the Muslim Rights Concern (MURIC). The Islamic human rights group gave the bill its blessing in a statement issued by its Executive Director, Profes

item.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.

item.title: RCCG Men Tell Tinubu To Prioritise Business Growth Over Government Jobs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Men of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG) have appealed to President Bola Tinubu-led federal government to create an enabling environment that will allow businesses to thrive, saying economic empowerment rather than dependence on government jobs remains the key to addressing hardship in the

item.title: Niger Police Command rescue 9 children from traffickers, one month old baby from abductors
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: …Arrests three suspects From John Adams, Minna The Niger State Police Command says it has rescued 11 female children from traffickers across the different locations in the state. Also the police said a one month old baby was rescued her abductors in Minna, the state capital The police command in a

item.title: NANS, NYSC To Advance Graduate Entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) and the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) have strengthened their institutional partnership aimed at promoting graduate entrepreneurship, skills development and youth empowerment across Nigeria. The students’ body concluded a high-level strategi

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: As political campaigns intensify ahead of the August 15 Osun State governorship election, infrastructure development has emerged as one of the major battlegrounds between Governor Ademola Adeleke and the opposition All Progressives Congress (APC). item.title: Photos: Burna Boy reunites with Shakira ahead of World Cup final halftime show
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Burna Boy has reunited with Colombian music sensation Shakira ahead of their performance at the inaugural FIFA World Cup final halftime show. item.title: Shettima Directs LGs To Lead Fight Against Malnutrition
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima has charged local government chairmen to drive the implementation of the Nutrition 774 (N-774) Initiative at the grassroots to improve nutritional outcomes in communities across Nigeria. He has thus directed the Association of Local Governments of Nigeria (ALGON) to ac

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governorship aspirant of the All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) in Bayelsa State, Dr Domor Mienye, has said the state cannot achieve long-term prosperity by relying on political approaches that have failed to produce broad economic growth over the past three decades. item.title: NPFL: Ojokojo appointed Kwara United chief coach
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kwara United have fortified their technical crew with the appointment of Ladie Ojokojo, DAILY POST reports. Ojokojo was drafted into the Harmony Boys technical crew from Kwara Football Academy. He will serve as the Chief coach of the team for the 2026-27 season. The young tactician is expected to re

item.title: Endemic Corruption Fuelling Insecurity In Nigeria, Varsity Don
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Professor of Criminology at the University of Uyo (UNIUYO), Ebere James Okorie, has identified endemic corruption in the Nigerian system as one of the major drivers of insecurity holding the nation down. He described the malaise as a deeply entrenched institutional virus that has weakened the gove

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Cross River State Governor Bassey Otu, has launched a statewide “Unite Against Terror” campaign as a decisive response to rising insecurity, which include kidnapping and other security threats troubling the state. The governor launched the campaign in Calabar during the inauguration of a

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Tems has now joined Wizkid as the only African artist to earn a Diamond single in US.

item.title: Ethiopian Drill explodes on the streets of Addis Ababa
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Last year, the aggressive genre of Drill could only be heard on social media in Ethiopia. But over the last few months the fast-paced, often matched with violent lyrics, style of hip hop has exploded on the streets of Addis Ababa, taking over mainstream bars and nightclubs. For some, its rapid popul

item.title: Messi breaks silence on Lamal’s baby picture
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, has called his picture with Lamine Yamal incredible on the eve of his country’s World Cup final against Spain. Messi had featured in a UNICEF event in Catalonia during his Barcelona days, and it included a photoshoot of the Argentine maestro bathing a baby in a tub.

item.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of tourist deaths linked to methanol
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Officials say they have no evidence of poisoning over the 2024 deaths because autopsies were not conducted.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia kill at least 8, wound dozens

item.description: Russian officials say strikes on retailer's warehouses caused civilian casualties; Kyiv says sites used to make drones.

item.title: Ethiopia: New refugee support centre opens in Addis Ababa
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The UN refugee agency and the Ethiopian government have opened a new support centre in Addis Ababa to provide refugees with free legal advice and access to essential services. The initiative aims to help refugees navigate bureaucracy, obtain vital documents and improve their chances of finding work.

item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested three suspects in connection with an alleged machete attack on an 18-year-old student of Government Day Secondary School, Miri, in the state.The student was reportedly attacked on July 15, 2026, while returning home at Birindi Sabon Kauran Lafiyari, Bauch

item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Fighters from the Tuareg separatist movement FLA and the Al-Qaeda-linked jihadist group JNIM temporarily seized the town on July 4 in the latest in a series of coordinated attacks in the West African country. item.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days taken to hospital
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Police in India have forcibly taken an activist to hospital after a 20-day hunger strike protesting the country's examination system. The case has drawn widespread attention and renewed debate over education reforms and the handling of public protests. France 24 correspondent Navodita Kumari reports

item.title: Why is Latin America shifting to the right?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
🇨🇴 🇵🇪 #Colombia and #Peru recently elected right-wing, #Trump-aligned leaders in close contests, which analysts link to weakening centrist parties and growing concern over crime rather than a clear ideological shift.

item.title: France cruise to second Nations Championship victory beating Japan 42-15

item.description: France cruised to victory in the final match of their Nations Championship southern hemisphere series beating Japan 42-15 to complete their three games with two wins and a loss.

item.title: EWA AT 5: Entertainment Week Africa Unveils Expanded Pan-African Edition for 2026
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Under the theme “Five Years of Closing the Gap,” EWA 2026 welcomes delegations from 18+ African countries and hosts the world premiere of University of Side Hustle, the first scripted feature from Livespot Studios, as the platform evolves from cultural gathering into the marketplace where Africa’s

item.title: Nigeria’s fuel puzzle deepens as imports surge, refinery output falls
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigeria’s importation of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), popularly known as petrol, increased by 207 percent in June 2026, according to new data released by the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA).

item.title: AIG urges cops in Gombe to uphold discipline, professionalism
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
AIG Kwaimoh charges Gombe State Police Command to uphold discipline, integrity, and professionalism. Learn how officers are urged to maintain ethical polic
Read More: https://punchng.com/aig-urges-cops-in-gombe-to-uphold-discipline-professionalism/

item.title: Super Eagles star Nathan Tella handed lifeline as new Bayer Leverkusen coach makes promise
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles winger Nathan Tella has been handed a major boost ahead of the new season after Bayer Leverkusen coach Carles Martínez assured him of an important role in his plans, Soccernet.ng reports. Tella endured a frustrating 2025/26 campaign at the BayArena, with injuries severely limiting his i

item.title: Mary Habila: Police spokesperson warns against allegations, urges restraints
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Uchenna Inya, Abakaliki The spokesperson of the Nigeria Police Force Headquarters Annex, Lagos, CSP Nkeiruka Nwode, has appealed to Nigerians to exercise restraint and refrain from spreading unverified allegations over the death of Miss Mary Habila. Habila died at the residence of the Minister

item.title: Past military feud starved soldiers of air support during operations — Bwala
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A past military rivalry between the Nigerian Army and Air Force led to soldiers being denied crucial air support during operations, according to presidenti
Read More: https://punchng.com/past-military-feud-starved-soldiers-of-air-support-during-operations-bwala/

item.title: Top 10 business hotels in Nigeria for 2026, ranked by guest ratings
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: For this ranking, Nairametrics assessed business hotels across Nigeria using TripAdvisor, widely regarded as the gold standard for hotel reviews due to its extensive database of user-generated ratings. The post Top 10 business hotels in Nigeria for 2026, ranked by guest ratings appeared first on Nai

item.title: Trump asks FIFA to award another World Cup to US, says leave out Canada, Mexico
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "You should choose the United States of America again. This time we will leave Canada and Mexico out," Trump said. The post Trump asks FIFA to award another World Cup to US, says leave out Canada, Mexico appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Tems becomes first African female artist to clinch Diamond single in US
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The platform wrote: “US Certifications (@RIAA): Future (@1future) Feat. @Drake & @temsbaby, WAIT FOR U 11x Platinum (11,000,000) ”. The post Tems becomes first African female artist to clinch Diamond single in US appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: FULL LIST: US warns citizens against travelling to 23 countries
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The United States Department of State has advised its citizens not to travel to 23 countries after placing them under its highest travel advisory, Level 4: Do Not Travel. According to an updated travel advisory published on Saturday on the department’s TravelGov X account, countries placed under Lev

item.title: Nigeria taps banks, local capital to close $171bn climate finance gap
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria is turning to domestic financial institutions and private capital to bridge a staggering $171 billion climate financing gap, as read more Nigeria taps banks, local capital to close $171bn climate finance gap

item.title: Opta supercomputer backs Spain to defeat Argentina in World Cup final
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Opta’s supercomputer has predicted Spain to defeat defending champions Argentina in Sunday’s 2026 FIFA World Cup final, giving La Roja a 59 per cent chance of lifting the trophy. According to TNT Sports, the data model rates Spain as favourites to win the title in New Jersey, while Argen

item.title: German foreign minister to visit Nigeria as Berlin seeks stronger trade, security ties
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Johann Wadephul, Germany’s Foreign Minister, will visit Nigeria next week for talks aimed at strengthening political, economic and security cooperation, read more German foreign minister to visit Nigeria as Berlin seeks stronger trade, security ties

item.title: FG denies plan to ban raw cocoa exports, pushes value addition
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Government has denied reports that it plans to ban the export of raw cocoa beans, stressing that its priority is to promote value addition across Nigeria's cocoa industry rather than restrict exports. The post FG denies plan to ban raw cocoa exports, pushes value addition appeared first
